programs.title: Team Building, Systems, Hiring - Mastermind Record
programs.description: This mastermind recording covers Team Building and Hiring, Incentives and Compensation, Content Creator and Affiliate Strategies

sections.name: main
sections.title: Team Building, Systems, Hiring - Mastermind Record

lessons.name: 7cbf5f4b
lessons.title: Dubai Mastermind Recording
lessons.description:
Introduction and Purpose of the Mastermind• Timestamps: 00:00:00 --> 00:00:49
Summary: The event, initially planned as a dinner meetup, evolved into a mastermind
session. Attendees include individuals ranging from seven-figure to eight-figure
monthly earners. The primary goal is for everyone to connect, mingle, and collectively
solve problems they face in their businesses.
Participant Introductions and Key Challenges• Timestamps: 00:01:27 --> 00:13:31
Discussion on Team Building and Hiring• Timestamps: 00:13:48 - 00:56:32
Summary: The main speaker (Spencer) and Shaun discuss best practices for team
building, emphasizing the importance of hiring the right people (not out of
desperation), and the two ways to build teams: bottom-up (common, starting with
junior roles) and top-down (hiring strategists/leaders first).
They stress the difficulty of finding good creative strategists and suggest either headhunting experienced individuals or hiring driven individuals and training them using their program.
They highlight the importance of clear expectations, KPIs, and investing time in developing
team members.
They also discuss how to retain talent through vision, culture, and fair compensation, noting that money isn't the only motivator.
The interview process, including loom videos, initial screenings, assessments, and reference checks, is detailed.
They also touch on the importance of continuous recruiting and being prepared to replace underperforming team members.
The discussion includes how to handle team members who are not improving and the importance of open communication and performance improvement plans.
Discussion on Incentives and Compensation• Timestamps: 00:56:32 - 01:12:28
Summary: The conversation shifts to incentivizing creative teams.
They discuss various models, including flat bonuses per winning ad, rolling percentages of ad spend, and cash bonuses based on overall business revenue goals.
The key is to make incentives clear, tangible, and aligned with company goals.
They emphasize that incentives should be a win-win for both the company and the employee.
They also discuss how non-monetary incentives like job titles, growth opportunities, and appreciation can be powerful motivators.
The example of an operations manager whose pay is scaled based on revenue is given to illustrate how to incentivize roles that don't directly generate revenue but are crucial for the company's smooth operation.
Content Creators and Affiliates• Timestamps: 01:16:31 - 01:42:51
Summary: The discussion moves to content creators and affiliates, particularly in the
context of UGC (User-Generated Content) and TikTok Shop.
They highlight that creators can act as strategists, copywriters, filmers, and editors all in one, and that UGC often yields better results than traditional ads.
They explain their strategy of product seeding and then hiring creators who produce winning ads on a retainer, with additional incentives based on ad spend or revenue.
The idea of using coupon codes for affiliates to automate payments and track performance is also explored.
They emphasize that the goal is to generate content that can be used for paid ads, effectively making money while acquiring content.
The importance of white-listing access for creators is mentioned.
The conversation also touches on the difference between UGC creators and traditional influencers, and how to leverage both for different purposes, with a focus on performance over follower count.
They discuss how to get creators to produce content by providing clear guidelines, hooks, and b-roll examples, while also allowing them creative freedom.
The concept of using TikTok Shop as a content generation engine, even if direct sales from it are not the primary goal, is also discussed.
